envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

identity.title: All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Business and institutional customers buy time, reports, opinions, inspections, or case work through hourly, project, retainer, per-engagement, and occasionally recurring-service contracts.
lens.deviation_from_default: Narrowed for coherence because 541990 is a residual category spanning materially different professional services; retained only repeat outsourced services delivered by transferable operating practices.
lens.excluded_activities: One-off expert engagements, owner-only practices without transferable operations, captive units, internal reorganizations, non-control financings, software-only providers, and activities classified to a specific NAICS such as translation, veterinary, legal, accounting, engineering, or management consulting.
lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market employer firms in this residual NAICS that provide a repeat outsourced professional service to external customers, including repeat appraisal, dispute-resolution, research, technical-review, and similar specialist engagements where the practice, rather than a named individual alone, can be transferred.

caveats: OEWS explains that its industry occupation estimates exclude self-employed workers, an important limitation for this code.
The injected compensation-to-receipts input may be misleading because the residual NAICS mixes labor models and its QCEW wage population need not match the selected repeat-service practices.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted OEWS occupation mix for 541990, paired with task inventories and time-and-motion interviews at 15 to 20 retained-practice firms, would test the exposed share.
a.rationale: The retained work is document, data, research, report, scheduling, and client-communication intensive, so AI can substitute or avoid hiring for a meaningful share of wages. It is not assumed to substitute the professional judgment, field observation, licensed sign-off, or adversarial/client-facing work that varies across the residual code.

caveats: The BLS market-research projection is an occupation-wide measure across many industries and includes work outside this lens.
Residual-code segments can have sharply different demand cycles and no exact constant-price demand series was retrieved.
d5.change_evidence: Five years of 541990 product-line receipts deflated by a service-price index, client procurement-spend panels, and segment-level backlog data would replace the adjacent occupation signal.
d5.rationale: Demand for specialized external analysis, market information, technical review, and accountable opinions should remain broadly stable, with growth in data use supporting some segments. As an imperfect adjacent indicator, BLS projects market-research analyst employment to rise 7 percent from 2024 to 2034, but that occupation is not a measure of this industry's constant-quality service quantity.

caveats: Census owner-characteristic data cover employer firms broadly, not this exact LMM eligible subset.
The injected LMM firm count uses a size-to-EBITDA bridge and does not establish whether a firm has recurring work or transferable operations.
e.change_evidence: A business-register sample matched to websites, service descriptions, revenue concentration, employee count, and recurring-engagement history would measure eligibility directly.
e.rationale: The code is explicitly a catch-all for professional, scientific, or technical services not classified elsewhere. A substantial but far from universal share is likely to be a repeat outsourced service with transferable operations; excluded solo experts, one-off engagements, and nonrepeat practices lower eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541990 ($27,219,781,409) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541990 ($35,548,824,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $261,287 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541990); class <5: 18,002 firms, avg revenue/firm $408,515 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $63,933 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,791 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,600,634 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $250,499 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 911 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,055,095 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $478,122 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 717 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,186,007 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,437,610 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 146 firms, avg revenue/firm $36,320,979 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,684,233 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 123 firms, avg revenue/firm $86,630,772 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,557,716 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 863

caveats: The source documents analyst duties and a broad employment outlook, not the probability that future 541990 service quantity requires an operator.
The answer varies substantially by specialty, regulation, client risk tolerance, and whether the service is a report or an accountable opinion.
o.change_evidence: Customer interviews on willingness to buy AI-only versus human-accountable deliverables, plus retained-practice service-line revenue by workflow, would identify the operator-required share.
o.rationale: Even where AI reduces research and document production, customers often need an accountable operator to frame scope, validate evidence, exercise professional judgment, communicate findings, and assume responsibility. The lower bound recognizes that standardized research, simple reports, and some intake can become self-served or software supplied.

caveats: Billing terms are not measured for this residual NAICS.
A practice with cost-plus or tightly benchmarked fees could retain far less than the stated range, while a scarce specialist may retain more.
q.change_evidence: Customer contracts, renewal price histories, fee schedules, and win-loss interviews from a representative set of retained practices would identify contractual pass-through and repricing.
q.rationale: Hourly and project engagements expose labor savings to customer renegotiation, and standardized research or reports are vulnerable to price competition. Retainers, regulated accountability, client relationships, and specialized interpretation allow the operator to retain part of an implemented benefit, yielding a middle range rather than full retention.

caveats: This is an operational adoption judgment, not a measured industry implementation rate.
Regulated and high-liability specialties inside the code may implement much more slowly than routine research and report-production practices.
rho.change_evidence: Firm-level AI deployment logs, before-and-after staffing data, and interviews with responsible licensed professionals and insurers would show which workflows reached production use.
rho.rationale: Drafting, summarization, data preparation, search, and workflow routing can be implemented in five years, but human review remains necessary where an opinion, confidential client record, regulatory duty, or field evidence controls the deliverable. The broad retained population makes implementation consistency uncertain.

caveats: BizBuySell's service-sector transactions are not NAICS 541990 and skew below the selected EBITDA band.
No retrieved source measures qualifying control transfers for the selected 541990 practices.
s5.change_evidence: Transaction records from PitchBook, Axial, GF Data, or local business brokers, plus owner-age and succession responses for 541990 LMM firms, would materially narrow this estimate.
s5.rationale: A five-year control-transfer probability is bounded above the annual transaction flow because succession can accumulate over five years, but below a simple retirement proxy because many professional practices depend on the owner and will close or internally reorganize. Census confirms that its owner survey captures owner age, while BizBuySell reports a live small-business service transaction market rather than an industry-specific transfer rate.

principal_driver: Repeat professional workflows contain document and data tasks that can be automated while a responsible operator still delivers the client outcome.
principal_weakness: NAICS 541990 is a catch-all category, so the available evidence does not measure the selected repeat-service practice population directly.
